description.en: A paper-thin, sour fermented flatbread of pure sorghum, left to ferment on a household starter for a day before being spread in one swift motion across a hot griddle. Sudan's daily bread, torn to scoop molokhia and okra stews.
description.de: Ein hauchdünnes, säuerliches fermentiertes Fladenbrot aus reinem Sorghum, das einen Tag lang auf einem Haushalts-Sauerteig fermentiert, bevor es in einer schnellen Bewegung auf einer heißen Platte verteilt wird. Sudans tägliches Brot, gerissen für Molokhia- und Okra-Eintöpfe.
description.es: Un pan plano fermentado, ácido y fino como el papel, hecho de sorgo puro, dejado fermentar sobre una masa madre casera durante un día antes de extenderse en un solo movimiento sobre una plancha caliente. El pan diario de Sudán, para mojar en guisos de molokhia y quingombó.
description.fr: Un pain plat fermenté, acidulé et fin comme du papier, fait de sorgho pur, laissé fermenter sur un levain familial pendant une journée avant d'être étalé en un geste rapide sur une plaque brûlante. Le pain quotidien du Soudan, pour saucer les ragoûts de molokhia et de gombo.

origin.note: Sudan's staple bread, and South Sudan's second staple after asida; the household starter, sometimes called al-rahiba, is passed down and fed between batches like a family heirloom.

ingredients.note: al-rahiba, a household starter culture, or a spoonful of a previous batch

yield.shape: large paper-thin round or rectangle sheet

keeping.note: Best fresh, still faintly warm and pliable; dries out within a couple of days, though some households sun-dry extra kisra for long-term storage and rehydrate it later.

method.title: Mix the ajin
method.summary: Stir sorghum flour and water together, with a spoonful of starter from a previous batch if you have one, into a smooth, thin batter.

method.title: Ferment
method.summary: Cover and leave in a warm spot for 12-24 hours, until visibly bubbly and pleasantly sour.

method.title: Season
method.summary: Stir in a little salt just before cooking.

method.title: Heat the griddle
method.summary: Heat a lightly greased flat griddle or saj until evenly hot.

method.title: Spread paper-thin
method.summary: Pour a ladleful on and spread it instantly with a scraper into a wide, paper-thin sheet.

method.title: Cook one side
method.summary: Cook on that one side only until the top turns spongy and porous and the underside is lightly spotted, about a minute.

method.title: Stack & serve
method.summary: Stack the sheets on a plate as you go, then tear and use to scoop stews.

source.adaptation: Source gave process and cultural detail but no precise quantities; flour-water ratio and salt are drawn from general convention (2 cups each), using an estimated 120 g/cup sorghum weight.
